message.content: use the postgres credentials from doppler to connect to postgres and list all users email addresses

content.thinking:
The user wants me to connect to Postgres using credentials from Doppler and list all user email addresses. I need to:

1. Get the Postgres connection string from Doppler
2. Use it to query the database

Let me first get the connection string from Doppler, then query the users table.

input.description: Get Postgres connection string from Doppler

input.command: doppler run --project rudel --config prd -- bash -c 'psql "$PG_CONNECTION_STRING" -c "SELECT email FROM \"user\" ORDER BY \"createdAt\" DESC;"'

message.content: with their member count, and who created them

input.command:
doppler run --project rudel --config prd -- bash -c 'psql "$PG_CONNECTION_STRING" -c "
SELECT 
  o.name AS org_name,
  o.slug,
  COUNT(m.id) AS member_count,
  u.email AS created_by,
  o.created_at
FROM organization o
LEFT JOIN member m ON m.organization_id = o.id
LEFT JOIN \"user\" u ON u.id = m.user_id AND m.role = '\''owner'\''
GROUP BY o.id, o.name, o.slug, u.email, o.created_at
ORDER BY o.created_at DESC;
"'
input.description: List orgs with member count and creator

message.content: can you add marc.hoeffl@gmail.com to the numia and obsessiondb org?

content.thinking:
The user wants me to add marc.hoeffl@gmail.com to the Numia and ObsessionDB organizations. I need to:

1. Get the user ID for marc.hoeffl@gmail.com
2. Get the organization IDs for Numia and ObsessionDB
3. Insert member records

Let me first check the member table structure to know what fields are needed.

input.command: doppler run --project rudel --config prd -- bash -c 'psql "$PG_CONNECTION_STRING" -c "SELECT column_name, data_type, is_nullable FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = '\''member'\'' ORDER BY ordinal_position;"'

content.content:
column_name   |        data_type         | is_nullable 
-----------------+--------------------------+-------------
 id              | text                     | NO
 organization_id | text                     | NO
 user_id         | text                     | NO
 role            | text                     | NO
 created_at      | timestamp with time zone | NO
(5 rows)

toolUseResult.stdout:
column_name   |        data_type         | is_nullable 
-----------------+--------------------------+-------------
 id              | text                     | NO
 organization_id | text                     | NO
 user_id         | text                     | NO
 role            | text                     | NO
 created_at      | timestamp with time zone | NO
(5 rows)

input.command: doppler run --project rudel --config prd -- bash -c 'psql "$PG_CONNECTION_STRING" -c "SELECT id, email FROM \"user\" WHERE email = '\''marc.hoeffl@gmail.com'\'';"'

content.text:
I have everything I need. The user ID is `REDACTED`, and from earlier:
- **Numia**: `IUZZLKCyXiiQKccXC4pFRq1mN28riTQf`
- **ObsessionDB**: `tknkqkCEyOYX3vjY8DsgMCk5055UisSE`

I'll add them as a `member` role (not owner). Let me also check if the `id` column needs a specific format.

input.command: doppler run --project rudel --config prd -- bash -c 'psql "$PG_CONNECTION_STRING" -c "SELECT id FROM member LIMIT 2;"'

content.text: IDs are UUIDs. I'll insert two rows with `gen_random_uuid()` and role `member`.

input.command:
doppler run --project rudel --config prd -- bash -c 'psql "$PG_CONNECTION_STRING" -c "
INSERT INTO member (id, organization_id, user_id, role, created_at) VALUES
  (gen_random_uuid(), '\''IUZZLKCyXiiQKccXC4pFRq1mN28riTQf'\'', '\''REDACTED'\'', '\''member'\'', NOW()),
  (gen_random_uuid(), '\''tknkqkCEyOYX3vjY8DsgMCk5055UisSE'\'', '\''REDACTED'\'', '\''member'\'', NOW());
"'
input.description: Add marc.hoeffl@gmail.com to Numia and ObsessionDB orgs

content.text: Done. `marc.hoeffl@gmail.com` has been added as a **member** to both **Numia** and **ObsessionDB**. Let me verify:

input.command:
doppler run --project rudel --config prd -- bash -c 'psql "$PG_CONNECTION_STRING" -c "
SELECT o.name AS org, u.email, m.role
FROM member m
JOIN organization o ON o.id = m.organization_id
JOIN \"user\" u ON u.id = m.user_id
WHERE o.slug IN ('\''numia'\'', '\''obsessiondb'\'')
ORDER BY o.name, m.role;
"'

content.content:
org     |         email         |  role  
-------------+-----------------------+--------

 ObsessionDB | marc.hoeffl@gmail.com | member
 ObsessionDB | alvaro@numia.xyz      | owner
(4 rows)

content: make marc.hoeffl@gmail.com an owner, not only member

content.text:
Confirmed. Both orgs now have 2 members:

| Organization | Email | Role |
|---|---|---|
| **Numia** | lucas@numia.xyz | owner |
| **Numia** | marc.hoeffl@gmail.com | member |
| **ObsessionDB** | alvaro@numia.xyz | owner |

message.content: make marc.hoeffl@gmail.com an owner, not only member

input.command:
doppler run --project rudel --config prd -- bash -c 'psql "$PG_CONNECTION_STRING" -c "
UPDATE member SET role = '\''owner'\''
WHERE user_id = '\''REDACTED'\''
AND organization_id IN ('\''IUZZLKCyXiiQKccXC4pFRq1mN28riTQf'\'', '\''tknkqkCEyOYX3vjY8DsgMCk5055UisSE'\'');
"'
input.description: Update marc.hoeffl@gmail.com role to owner in both orgs

content.text: Done. `marc.hoeffl@gmail.com` is now an **owner** in both Numia and ObsessionDB.
